Statement3: Perhaps people whose relationship to the world is psychologically painful choose such glasses to transmit inforamtion that they are painful Statement4: At any rate, it can be concluded that when such glasses are worn, it is because the wearer has a tendency to be depressed or hypochondriacal.
train_3160: Statement1: Newspaper editorial : many pharmaceutical companies develop" me too" drugs, drugs designed to duplicate, more or less, the effect of another company's product that is already on the market. Statement2: Some critics object that such development constitutes an unnecessary, redundant use of resources that provides no new benefits to consumers. However, the entry of" me too" drugs into the market can result in a price reduction for the drugs they resemble. Statement4: Therefore, " me too" drugs can indeed damage the development of new drugs.
train_522: Statement1: The ergonomic society conducted a study that indicated that many people develop severe back problems during adulthood, and that virtually all such people who received chiropractic treatment showed great improvement. Statement2: Therefore, in order to minimize the proportion of the population that suffers from back pain, the ergonomic society recommended that chiropractic treatment be directed toward those people who suffer from any back problems.
train_2039: Statement1: Because a large disparity in pay between the public and private sectors has developed in recent years, many experienced and extremely capable government administrators have quit their posts and taken positions in private-sector management. Statement2: Government will be able to recapture these capable administrators by widening the disparity in pay to a level comparable to those of the private sector. Statement3: In that way, the functioning of public agencies will be improved.
train_1834: Statement1: Editorialist : some people propose that, to raise revenues and encourage conservation, our country's taxes on oil, gasoline, and coal should be increased. Statement2: However, if such a tax increase is equal to all enegy, it would do more harm than good transportation. Statement3: . Statement4: Many families would be unfairly burdened with higher transportation costs. Statement5: Finally, by reducing the demand for energy, the tax increase would reduce the number of energy production jobs.
train_3775: Statement1: A drug that is highly effective in treating many types of infection can, at present, be obtained only from the bark of the ibora, a tree that is quite rare in the wild. It takes the bark of 5,000 trees to make one kilogram of the drug. Statement3: It follows, therefore, that continued production of the drug must inevitably lead to the cost reduction of drug.
train_1440: Statement1: Geologist : a geological fault in the mountain under which the proposed nuclear waste storage facility would be buried could, after a few thousand years, cause the contents to seep out or water to seep in. Since nuclear waste remains dangerous for up to 25,000 years, such seepage would be disastrous. Statement3: However we could place a nuclear waste storage facility under this mountain before scientists investigate whether this mountain has any geological faults.
train_1623: Statement1: For over two centuries, no one had been able to make damascus blades-blades with a distinctive serpentine surface pattern -because the sword makers died in the war for scrambling the only iron resource of that time. But a contemporary sword maker may just have rediscovered how. Statement2: Using iron with trace impurities, this contemporary sword maker seems to have finally hit on an intricate process by which he can produce a blade indistinguishable from a true damascus blade.
train_2134: Statement1: There are two supposedly conflicting hypotheses as to what makes for great national leaders : one is that such leaders successfully shape public opinion, and the other is that they are adept at reacting to it. However, treating these hypotheses as mutually exclusive is evidently a mistake. Statement3: All leaders who have had successfully shape public opinion have been adroit in reacting to public opinion.
train_906: Statement1: Editor : Statement2: Many candidates say that if elected they will reduce governmental intrusion into voters'lives. Voters rarely elect politicians who instead promise that the government will provide assistance to solve their most pressing problems. Statement4: Because governmental assistance, however, costs money, and money can come only from taxes, which can be considered a form of governmental intrusion. Statement5: However, governmental intrusion into the lives of voters will rarely be substantially reduced over time in a democracy.
train_1073: Statement1: Economist : technology now changes so rapidly that workers need periodic retraining. Such retraining can be most efficient only if it allows individual companies to meet their own shortterm needs. Statement3: Hence, no single type of retraining program allows individual companies to meet their own shortterm needs
train_2174: Statement1: Researchers hope to find clues about the a'mk peoples who lived about one thousand years ago but who left few obvious traces. Statement2: The researchers plan to hire the few remaining shamans of the modern-day indigenous people in kaumpta, who are believed to be descended from the a'mk, to lead them to ancestral sites that may be the remains of a'mk buildings or ceremonial spaces. Statement3: The shamans were taught the location of such sites as part of their traditional training as youths, and their knowledge of traditional kaumpta customs may help determine the nature of any sites the researchers find.
train_165: Statement1: Many important types of medicine have been developed from substances discovered in plants that grow only in tropical rain forests. Statement2: There are thousands of plant species in these rain forests that have not yet been studied by scientists, and it is very likely that many such plants also contain substances of medicinal value. Statement3: Thus, if the tropical rain forests are preserved, substance of medicinal value contained in plant species will be discovered.
train_1755: Statement1: Over the last five years, every new major alternative-energy initiative that initially was promised government funding has since seen that funding severely curtailed. In no such case has the government come even close to providing the level of funds initially earmarked for these projects. Statement3: Although large corporations have made it a point to discourage alternative-energy projects, corporations'actions might not influence the government's funding decisions.
train_492: Statement1: Reducing stress lessens a person's sensitivity to pain. Statement2: This is the conclusion reached by researchers who played reducing stress audiotapes to patients before they underwent surgery and afterward while they were recovering. Statement3: One tape consisted of conversation ; the other consisted of music. Statement4: Those who listened only to the tape showed more stress during surgery and afterward than those who took anesthesia, painkillers and listen to tape.
train_3850: Statement1: There are many structural and thematic similarities between piers plowman by langland ( 1330-1400 ) and house of fame by chaucer ( 1342-1400 ), two middle english poems relating dream visions. Statement2: Some critics have argued that because a number of the shared elements are uncommon in middle english poetry, and because chaucer's poem probably predates langland's by a few years, langland was most likely influenced by house of fame when writing piers plowman.
train_3770: Statement1: Each of two drugs, s and t, greatly reduces the effects of potentially fatal heart attacks if given as soon as possible after the attack begins, but a trial has shown that use of drug t instead of drug s would prevent death in one additional case out of 120. Statement2: Drug t, however, costs $ 2,000 more per treatment than drug s. Statement3: Therefore society is presented with a stark policy decision : whether or not to pay the $ 240,000 it would cost to use drug t in order to make sure to save patient.
train_4445: Statement1: Medical researcher : scientists compared a large group of joggers who habitually stretch before jogging to an equal number of joggers who do not stretch before jogging. Statement2: Both groups of joggers incurred different number of injuries. Statement3: This indicates that stretching before jogging does help to prevent injuries.
train_1929: Statement1: Medical researcher : as expected, records covering the last four years of ten major hospitals indicate that babies born prematurely were more likely to have low birth weights and to suffer from health problems than were babies not born prematurely. Statement2: These records also indicate that mothers who had received adequate prenatal care were less likely to have low birth weight babies than were mothers who had received inadequate prenatal care. Statement3: Indaequate prenatal care, therefore, significantly causes the low birth weight babies.
train_1539: Statement1: Magazine article : sugar consumption may exacerbate attention deficit disorder ( add ) in children. Statement2: A recent study found that children produce large amounts of adrenaline within hours after consuming large amounts of sugar. This increase in adrenaline is especially noticeable if the source of sugar is candy, in which case the candy's effects are not ameliorated by the ingestion of other foodstuffs.
train_816: Statement1: Traditional hatcheries raise fish in featureless environments and subject them to dull routines, whereas new, experimental hatcheries raise fish in visually stimulating environments with varied routines. When released into the wild, fish from the experimental hatcheries are bolder than those from traditional hatcheries in exploring new environments and trying new types of food. Statement3: Fish raised in the experimental hatcheries, therefore, are more similar to the fish in the wild.
train_4578: Statement1: People with higher-than-average blood levels of a normal dietary by-product called homocysteine are twice as likely to be diagnosed with alzheimer's disease as are those with average or below-average homocysteine levels. Statement2: However, it is likely that the risk of developing alzheimer's disease could not be reduced by including in one's diet large amounts of b vitamins and folic acid, which convert homocysteine into substances known to have no relation to alzheimer's disease because they will be metabolized by the body easily
train_1936: Statement1: In a transportation company, a certain syndrome often attributed to stress by medical experts afflicts a significantly higher percentage of workers in department f than in any other department. Statement2: We can conclude, , that the work may done not in department f subjects workers to higher stress levels than does the work in the other departments in the company.
train_806: Statement1: In mountainous regions, the timberline is the highest altitude at which trees grow. Statement2: In the rocky mountains, the current timberline is at the altitude above which growing season temperatures remain cooler than 10 degrees centigrade. Statement3: Fossilized remains of the same species of trees that grew 10,000 years ago have been found 100 meters above the current rocky mountain timberline. Statement4: Clearly, therefore, the climate of the rockymountains during this 10,000 years iscooler than it was 10,000 years ago.
train_4198: Statement1: The population of peregrine falcons declined rapidly during the 1950's and 1960's and reached an all-time low in the early 1970's. Statement2: Scientists attribute the decline to peregrine falcons abandoning eggs, whole or not, that fall out of the nest.
train_3982: Statement1: The reason a composer is remembered is not that he had a high ratio of outstanding compositions to mediocre compositions. It is rather because he was such a prolific composer. Statement3: If he wrote more than a thousand full-fledged compositions, so it was inevitable that some of them would be outstanding and, being outstanding, survive the ages.
train_2978: Statement1: Most small children are flat-footed. Statement2: This failure of the foot to assume its natural arch, if it persists past early childhood can sometimes result in discomfort and even pain later in life. Statement3: Traditionally, flat-footedness in children has been treated by having all the children wear special shoes that give extra support to the foot, in order to correct the flat-footedness.
train_3425: Statement1: A survey of entrepreneurs who started companies last year shows that while virtually all did substantial preparatory research and planning, only half used that work to produce a formal business plan. Statement2: Although, on average, the entrepreneurs without formal plans secured the capital they needed in half the time of those with plans, these survey results do not indicate that, in fact, formal plans help the entrepreneurs who produced them to secure the profit they needed.
train_3237: Statement1: On the caribbean island of guadeloupe, a researcher examined 35 patients with atypical parkinson's disease and compared their eating habits to those of 65 healthy adults. Statement2: She found that all of the patients with atypical parkinson's regularly ate the tropical fruits soursop, custard apple, and pomme cannelle, but 10 of the healthy adults regularly ate even more. Statement3: From this, she couldn't conclude that eating these fruits causes atypical parkinson's.
train_476: Statement1: In the nation of partoria, large trucks currently have a much higher rate of traffic accidents per mile driven than other vehicles do. Statement2: However, the large trucks-those with two or three trailers-had less than a third of the accident rate of single--trailer trucks. Statement3: Clearly, therefore, one way for partoria to prevent all traffic accidents would be to require shippers to use large trucks.
train_4286: Statement1: Gilbert : this food label is mistaken. It says that these cookies contain only natural ingredients, but they contain alphahydroxy acids that are chemically synthesized by the cookie company at their plant. Statement3: Sabina : the label is not mistaken. Statement5: After all, alphahydroxy also are just a part of the manufacturing process.
train_2999: Statement1: Feathers recently taken from seabirds stuffed and preserved in the 1880s have been found to contain only half as much mercury as feathers recently taken from living birds of the same species, as the decrease of the amount of mercury is considered. Statement2: Since mercury that accumulates in a seabird's feathers as the feathers grow is derived from fish eaten by the bird, these results indicate that mercury levels in the ocean habitat of saltwater fish are higher now than they were 100 years ago.
train_1665: Statement1: Caffeine produced for plant species own defense can kill or inhibit the growth of the larvae of several species of insects. Statement2: One recent experiment showed that tobacco hornworm larvae die when they ingest a preparation that consists, in part, of finely powdered tea leaves, which contain caffeine. Statement3: This result is evidence for the hypothesis that the presence of non-negligible quantities of caffeine in various parts of tobacco plant is not accidental but evolved as a defense for it.
train_2862: Statement1: Political advertisement : mayor delmont's critics complain about the jobs that were lost in the city under delmont's leadership. Yet the fact is that not only were more jobs created than were eliminated, but the average pay for these new jobs has been higher than the average pay for jobs citywide every year since delmont took office. Statement3: So there can be no question that throughout delmont's tenure the average paycheck in this city has been getting steadily from a ten-year low.
train_4199: Statement1: Government official : currently many business travelers rely on a one-hour flight between the cities, but complain about congestion and delays at the airport. Given this option, no significant recessions will happen in aplandia, since it will allow business travelers to travel between the city centers in less time than a flight with the expected delays plus the commute to and from airports would take. Statement2: The new high-speed railway between hamport and dudley, aplandia 's two major financial hubs, will be able to make the same trip in slightly less than three hours.
train_1456: Statement1: If the government increases its funding for civilian scientific research, civilian scientific will believe that such research has become primarily the government's responsibility. Statement2: When they believe that research is government's primarily, civilian scientific research will increase their fund requirement toward research. Statement3: Therefore, in order to maintain the overall level of funding for civilian scientific research, the government should increase its own funding.
train_171: Statement1: Chronic fatigue syndrome is characterized by prolonged fatigue, muscular pain, and neurological problems. Statement2: It is not known whether these symptoms are all caused by a single virus or whether each symptom is the result of a separate viral infection. Statement3: A newly synthesized drug that targets the nervous system has been tested on those who suffer from chronic fatigue syndrome. Although the specific antiviral effects of this drug are unknown, it has lessened the severity of all of the symptoms of chronic fatigue syndrome. Statement5: Thus there is evidence that chronic fatigue syndrome is, in fact, related to nervous system.
train_583: Statement1: An overwhelming proportion of the few most productive employees at saleco's regional offices work not eight hours a day, five days a week, as do other saleco employees, but rather only work in the early afternoon, four days a week, with friday off. Statement3: Noting this phenomenon, saleco's president plans to increase overall productivity by keeping the offices closed on fridays and having all employees work the same schedule-only work in the early afternoon, four days a week.
train_2008: Statement1: Lawmaker : raising taxes is not the only means of reducing government debt. The government's stockpile of helium is worth 25 percent more, at current market prices, than the debt accumulated in acquiring and storing it. Statement3: Therefore, by selling the helium, the government can not only pay off that debt but reduce a large proportion of its overall debt as well.
train_682: Statement1: Automobile emissions are a significant source of air pollutants, and cars over five years old typically generate significantly greater amounts of pollutants than newer cars. In torinia, which has recently built its first automobile manufacturing plant, most cars are over five years old. Statement3: Aiming to boost torinia's economy and reduce air pollution, the government plans to introduce incentives for torinians to scrap their old cars every five years and replace them with electric car.
train_2684: Statement1: Scientist : it seems likely that the earliest dinosaurs to fly did so by lifting off the ground from a running start rather than, as some scientists think, by gliding out of trees. Statement2: Animals gliding from trees are able to fly with very simple wings. Such wings represent evolutionary middle stages toward developing the large wings that we associate with flying dinosaurs.
train_160: Statement1: A recent study showed that people who address problems quickly and directly are significantly more likely to have gum disease than are people who react to problems by refusing to think about them. Statement2: Since stress can have a negative effect on the immune system, the study's results clearly indicate that some forms of gum disease are caused or aggravated by suppression of the immune system.
train_686: Statement1: The use of growth-promoting antibiotics in hog farming can weaken their effectiveness in treating humans because such use can spread resistance to those antibiotics among microorganisms. But now the smee company, one of the largest pork marketers, may stop buying pork raised on feed containing antibiotics to which microorganisms have developed resistence. Statement3: Smee has 60 percent of the pork market, and farmers who sell to smee would certainly stop using antibiotics in order to avoid jeopardizing their sales. Statement4: So if smee makes this change, it will probably significantly slow the decline in antibiotics'effectiveness for humans.
train_3128: Statement1: Donations of imported food will be distributed to children in famine-stricken countries in the form of free community meals. Statement2: The process is is not efficient and will lose the standing of the political party's votes because the children are easy to reach at the schools and cooking facilities are often available on site.
train_4434: Statement1: The imposition of quotas limiting imported steel will help the big american steel mills. Statement2: By contrast, the quotas will not help" mini-mills" flourish in the united states. Statement3: Those small domestic mills won't take more business from the big americal steel mills than would have been taken by the foreign steel mills in the absence of quotas.
train_2772: Statement1: In gandania, where the government has a monopoly on tobacco sales, the incidence of smoking-related health problems has risen steadily for the last twenty years. The health secretary recently proposed a series of laws aimed at export all tobacco in gandania. Statement3: Profits from tobacco sales, , account for ten percent of gandania's annual revenues. Statement4: Therefore, gandania can afford to institute the proposed laws.
train_1997: Statement1: Botanist : in an experiment, scientists raised domesticated radishes in a field with wild radishes, which are considered weeds. Within several generations, the domesticated radishes began to show the same flower color as the wild ones. Statement3: This suggests that resistance to pesticides, which is often a genetically engineered trait, may hard to be passed from domesticated crop plants to their relatives that are considered weeds.
train_2068: Statement1: Reconstructing ships so that they have a double hull can protect them from serious damage in the roughly 80 percent of shipping accidents that unavoidably result from severe weather conditions. Avoidable human error, causes about 20 percent of shipping accidents. Statement3: It is therefore much more cost-effective to reduce damage to ships by rebuilding them than to retrain sailors.
train_3656: Statement1: Because ethylene dibromide, a chemical used to fumigate grain, was blamed for the high rate of nerve damage suffered by people who work in grain-processing plants, many such plants switched to other chemical fumigants two years ago. Statement2: Since then, however, the percentage of workers at these plants who were newly diagnosed with nerve damage has not dropped significantly. Statement3: Therefore, either ethylene dibromide was wrongly blamed or else the other chemicals also cause nerve damage.
train_1258: Statement1: Editorial : a proposed new law would limit elementary school class sizes to a maximum of 20 students. Most parents support this measure and argue that making classes smaller allows teachers to devote more time to each student, with the result that students become more engaged in the learning process. Statement3: However, researchers who conducted a recent study conclude from their results that this reasoning is questionable. Statement4: The researchers studied schools that had undergone recent reductions in class size, and found that despite an increase in the amount of time teachers spent individually with students, the students'engagement in the learning process decreased.
train_592: Statement1: Air traffic controllers and nuclear power plant operators are not allowed to work exceptionally long hours, because to do so would jeopardize lives. Statement2: Yet physicians in residency training are typically required to work 80-hour weeks. Statement3: The aforementioned restrictions on working exceptionally long hours can not be applied to resident physicians, since they are engaged in work of a life-or-death nature.
train_1543: Statement1: Psychologist : there are theories that posit completely different causal mechanisms from those posited by freudian psychological theory and that are more successful at predicting human behavior. Statement2: Therefore, freudian theories of behavior, no matter how suggestive or complex they are, ought to be abandoned in to stop studying falsetheories.
train_3737: Statement1: Editorial : the most vocal proponents of the proposed law are not permanent residents of this island but rather most of the summer residents, who leave when the vacation months have passed. Statement2: These people will benefit from passage of this law while not having to deal with the problems associated with its adoption. Statement3: Therefore, anyone who supports the proposed law is serving only the interests of the outsiders at the risk of creating problems for the island's permanent residents.
train_186: Statement1: One reliable clue as to which geographical regions an ancient relic was moved through in the past involves the analysis of pollen that clings to the surface of the relic, if this analysis is not painstaking. Statement2: A relic is linked to a geographical area by the identification of pollen from plants that are known to have been unique to that area.
train_1325: Statement1: A recent study confirms that nutritious breakfasts make workers less productive. Statement2: For one month, workers at plant a received free nutritious breakfasts every day before work, while workers in plant b did not. Statement3: The productivity of plant b's workers increased, while that of plant a's workers did not.
train_3004: Statement1: Historian : leibniz, the seventeenth-century philosopher, published his version of calculus before newton did. Statement2: But then newton revealed his private notebooks, which showed he had been using these ideas for at least a decade before leibniz's publication. Newton also claimed that he had disclosed these ideas to leibniz in a letter shortly before leibniz's publication. Statement4: Yet close examination of the letter shows that newton's few cryptic remarks did not reveal anything important about calculus. Statement5: Thus, leibniz and newton first discovered calculus.
train_4393: Statement1: Dietary researcher : a recent study reports that laboratory animals that were fed increased-calorie diets lived longer than laboratory animals whose caloric intake was not reduced. Statement2: In response, some doctors are advocating increased-calorie diets, in the belief that north americans'life spans can thereby be extended. However, this conclusion is not supported. Statement4: Laboratory animals tend to eat much less than animals in their natural habitats, which leads to their having a shorter life expectancy. Increasing their diets merely brings their caloric intake back to natural, optimal levels and reinstates their normal life spans.
train_225: Statement1: The caterpillar of the monarch butterfly feeds on milkweed plants, whose toxins make the adult monarch poisonous to many predators. The viceroy butterfly, whose caterpillars do not feed on milkweed plants, is very similar in appearance to the monarch. Statement3: Therefore, it can be concluded that the most of butterfly species is so seldom preyed on because of their visual resemblance to the monarch.
train_3817: Statement1: At one time, many astronomers assumed that earth remains motionless while the stars revolve around it. They reasoned that if the stars were farther away, they mustn't move at the same speed in order to circle earth during the day and reappear in roughly the same positions each night. Statement2: They concluded from this that the stars were not more than a few million miles from earth.
train_3633: Statement1: Early in the twentieth century, lake konfa became very polluted. Statement2: Recently fish populations have recovered as release of industrial pollutants has declined and the lake's waters have become cleaner. Statement3: Fears are now being voiced that the planned construction of an oil pipeline across the lake's bottom might cause harm to the lake and cause the fish population to decline again. Therefore, provided this technology is effective, those fears are groundless. Statement4: However, a technology for neutralizing pollution is being installed.
train_4525: Statement1: In two months, the legal minimum wage in the country of kirlandia will increase from five kirlandic dollars(kd5.00 ) opponents of this increase have argued that the resulting rise in wages will drive the inflation rate up. Statement2: Per hour to kd5. Statement3: 50 per hour. Statement5: In fact its impact on wages will probably great for the next year, since very large proportion of all kirfandic workers are currently receiving less than kd5. Statement6: 50 per hour.
train_2763: Statement1: It is repeatedly claimed that the dumping of nuclear waste poses no threat to people living nearby. If this claim could be made with certainty, there would be no reason for not locating sites in areas of dense population. Statement3: Thus the policy of dumping nuclear waste only in the more sparsely populated regions is meaningless despite some misgiving about safety on the part of those responsible for policy.
train_937: Statement1: Fast-food restaurants make up 45 percent of all restaurants in cantaria. Customers at these restaurants tend to be young ; in fact, studies have shown that the older people get, the less likely they are to eat in fast-food restaurants. Statement3: Since the average age of the canatrian population is gradually rising and will continue to do so, the number of all restaurants is likely to decrease.
train_1581: Statement1: Biologist : some computer scientists imagine that all that is required for making an artificial intelligence is to create a computer program that encapsulates the information contained in the human genome. They are mistaken. Statement3: The operation of the human brain is governed by the interactions of proteins whose structures are encoded in the human genome, which is too large to be copied.
train_978: Statement1: Archaeologists excavating a neanderthal campsite found discarded gazelle teeth and some tools used by nomadic people. Coincidentally, the archaeologists cite a neighboring campsite of nomadic cro-magnons that contained teeth from gazelles all killed by that kind of tools Statement2: The archaeologists concluded that the neanderthals had inhabited with the cro-magnons and thus were nomadic.
train_1347: Statement1: Nutritionist : vitamins synthesized by chemists are exactly the same as vitamins that occur naturally in foods. Statement2: However, it is not a waste of money to pay extra for brands of vitamin pills that are actually made of higher-quality ingredients or more natural ingredients than other brands are.
train_1312: Statement1: Archaeologist : researchers excavating a burial site in cyprus found a feline skeleton lying near a human skeleton. Statement2: Both skeletons were in the same sediment at the same depth and equally well-preserved, suggesting that the feline and human were buried together about 9,500 years ago. Statement3: However, this doesn't show that felines were domesticated around the time farming began, because they would have been useless in protecting stores of grain from mice.
train_4407: Statement1: Because of the lucrative but illegal trade in rhinoceros horns, a certain rhinoceros species has been hunted nearly to extinction. Statement2: But an effective way to ensure the survival of that species cannot be to periodically trim off the horns of all rhinoceroses, which will encourage the motivation for poaching.
train_3811: Statement1: No human will ever be able to do everything that some computers can do, for there is no problem that can not be solved by following any set of mechanically applicable rules. Statement2: Yet computers can solve problems by following some set of mechanically applicable rules.
train_487: Statement1: Julia joined michael scott paperless company, a small new york based tech startup company, last week. Statement2: Michael scott paperless recently received a valuation of ten million dollars. Statement3: Julia is clearly not the reason for the valuation.
train_527: Statement1: When butterfat was considered nutritious and healthful, a law was enacted requiring that manufacturers use the term" imitation butter" to indicate butter whose butterfat content had been diminished through the addition of water. Today, it is known that the high cholesterol content of butterfat makes it harmful to human health. Statement3: Since the public should be encouraged to eat foods with lower rather than higher butterfat content and since the term" imitation" with its connotations of falsity detersall the people who need to reduce their intake of cholesterol from purchasing products so designated, manufacturers who wish to give reduced-butterfat butter the more appealing name of" lite butter" should be allowed to do so.
train_4372: Statement1: Jane : television programs and movies that depict violence among teenagers are extremely popular. Statement2: Given how influential these media are, we have good reason to believe that these depictions will not cause young people to engage in violent behavior. Statement3: Hence, depictions of violence among teenagers should not be prohibited from movies and television programs, if only in those programs and movies promoted to young audiences. Statement4: Maurice : but you are recommending society without censorship! Besides which, your claim that television and movie depictions of violence can not cause violence is mistaken : violence among grown people postdates movies and television by centuries.
train_604: Statement1: Paleontologist : plesiosauromorphs were gigantic, long-necked marine reptiles that ruled the oceans during the age of the dinosaurs. Statement2: Most experts believe that plesiosauromorphs lurked and quickly ambushed their prey. Statement3: However, plesiosauromorphs must hunted by chasing their prey over long distances. Statement4: Because plesiosauromorph fins were quite long and thin, like the wings of birds specialized for long-distance flight.
train_477: Statement1: Advertisement : clark brand-name parts are made for cars manufactured in this country. Statement2: They satisfy all of our government automotive tests which proves clark brand parts are the best in the world. Statement3: With foreign-made parts, you never know which might be reliable and which are cheap look-alikes that are poorly constructed and liable to cost you hundreds of dollars in repairs. Statement4: Therefore, be smart and insist on parts which satisfied our government standards for your car.
train_1422: Statement1: The milk of many mammals contains cannabinoids, substances that are known to stimulate certain receptors in the brain. Statement2: To investigate the function of cannabinoids, researchers injected newborn mice with a chemical that is known to offset cannabinoids. Statement3: The injected mice showed far less interest in feeding their mothers' milk than normal newborn mice do. Statement4: Therefore, cannabinoids probably function to stimulate the appetite.
train_4480: Statement1: The town of stavanger, norway, was quiet and peaceful until the early 1960's, when stavanger became norway's center for offshore oil exploration. Between then and now, traffic noise in stavanger have greatly increased. Statement3: Clearly, this social problem is among the results of stavanger's oil boom.
train_24: Statement1: The conventional process for tanning leather uses large amounts of calcium oxide and sodium sulfide. Statement2: Tanning leather using biological catalysts' weight about the same as using these conventional chemicals if the weight of waste disposal is left out of the comparison. However, no less waste is produced with biological catalysts, and waste disposal is a substantial part of the overall cost of tanning. Statement4: It is therefore less costly to tan leather if biological catalysts are used instead.
train_3820: Statement1: Educational theorist : recent editorials have called for limits on the amount of homework assigned to children. Clearly, therefore, there is no need to impose the limits these editorials are calling for. Statement2: They point out that free-time activities play an important role in childhood development and that large amounts of homework reduce children's free time, hindering their development. But the average homework about skills or information that they can use in their academic work time for a ten year old, for example, is little more than 30 minutes per night.
train_1426: Statement1: Consumer advocate : businesses are typically motivated primarily by the desire to make as great a profit as possible, and advertising helps businesses to achieve this goal. But it is clear that the motive of maximizing profits does not impel businesses to present accurate information in their advertisements.
